reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_61]]
Speaker 1: In fact, we were one of the first two universities to launch fully online degree programs.

[[BLOCK:srt_62]]
Speaker 1: Since then, we’ve continued to grow and evolve, becoming a well-established institution with mature systems and strong operations.

[[BLOCK:srt_63]]
Speaker 1: Today, we offer online degree programs to students all over the world.

[[BLOCK:srt_64]]
Speaker 1: More importantly, Golden Gate University has always stayed ahead of the curve—anticipating future trends, embracing new challenges, and having the courage to take risks.

[[BLOCK:srt_65]]
Speaker 1: We are also one of the very few universities authorized to confer degrees in languages other than English.

[[BLOCK:srt_66]]
Speaker 1: Among U.S. institutions, only a small number are able to grant degrees in Mandarin.

[[BLOCK:srt_67]]
Speaker 1: For us, what matters most is making knowledge truly accessible and ensuring that students fully understand what they are learning. The most effective way to achieve that is to teach in a student’s native language.

[[BLOCK:srt_68]]
Speaker 1: We care about mastery of the subject matter—not whether you happen to speak a particular language fluently.

[[BLOCK:srt_69]]
Speaker 3: Exactly.

[[BLOCK:srt_70]]
Speaker 3: That’s what it means to be a true pioneer.

[[BLOCK:srt_71]]
Speaker 3: Mm.

[[BLOCK:srt_72]]
Speaker 2: That’s a great point.
